title: Under-fire-glazed Ethiopian Pork Chops Tagine
description: A nutty and flaky dish featuring pork chops, inspired by Ethiopian flavors and cooked in a traditional Kazan cauldron.
instructions: Season the pork chops with salt and black pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in the Kazan cauldron over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork chops for 2-3 minutes on each side, then set aside.
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in the cauldron, then add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the grated ginger, ground cinnamon, ground cardamom, and ground allspice to the cauldron and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the red wine and water, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the cauldron.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Return the pork chops to the cauldron and simmer, covered, until they reach an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C), about 15-20 minutes.
Remove the pork chops from the cauldron and place them in an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Strain the cooking liquid and discard the solids, then reduce the liquid to a glaze by cooking it over high heat until it thickens, about 5 minutes.
Brush the glaze over the pork chops and serve hot.
